candidate_problems.text: Can regulation achieve effective oversight without reproducing epistemic dependence on the regulated sector, and if so what concrete institutional designs generate genuinely independent knowledge production at acceptable cost and speed?

Is the conjectured incompatibility real or overstated: are independence and dense coordination actually mutually undermining, or can some sectors sustain stable arrangements where close contact improves performance without recurrent capture?

What empirical markers would distinguish 'capture displaced into subtler channels' from legitimate expert convergence, and how can we measure when industry categories, models, and personnel pipelines have become load-bearing determinants of regulatory judgment?
